name: Polydeuces
description: Polydeuces, also known as Pollux, was one of the twin brothers Castor and Polydeuces (collectively known as the Dioscuri) in Greek and Roman mythology. His mother was Leda, but he was fathered by Zeus, who seduced Leda in the guise of a swan. This divine parentage granted Polydeuces immortality, a trait that distinguished him from his mortal brother Castor, whose father was Tyndareus, the king of Sparta. Polydeuces was renowned for his exceptional boxing skills and strength, making him a formidable warrior. He participated in numerous adventures, including the Calydonian Boar hunt and the voyage of the Argonauts. His most significant deed was his unwavering loyalty and love for his brother Castor. When Castor was mortally wounded in a dispute over cattle, Polydeuces pleaded with Zeus to allow him to share his immortality with his brother. Zeus, moved by their devotion, granted them the option to spend alternate days on Mount Olympus with the gods and in the underworld with Hades. Eventually, they were transformed into the constellation Gemini, forever together in the night sky. Polydeuces symbolizes brotherly love, athleticism, and the acceptance of fate, even when it involves sacrifice. His immortal status and association with Zeus elevated him to a figure of reverence, particularly among sailors, who invoked the Dioscuri for protection at sea. Temples and shrines were dedicated to the Dioscuri throughout the ancient Greek world, highlighting their enduring importance in religious and cultural life. He represents the ideal of a hero who values kinship and is willing to make the ultimate sacrifice for a loved one, even at the cost of personal happiness.
subtitle: Immortal Boxer, Brother of Castor

keyMyths.title: The Birth of the Dioscuri
keyMyths.description: Leda laid two eggs, from which Castor and Clytemnestra emerged from one, and Polydeuces and Helen from the other. Polydeuces' birth from an egg fathered by Zeus granted him immortality.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Castor
keyMyths.description: Castor was killed in a cattle raid by Idas. Polydeuces avenged his brother's death but was inconsolable until Zeus allowed them to share immortality.

keyMyths.title: Becoming Gemini
keyMyths.description: To honor their brotherly love, Zeus placed Castor and Polydeuces in the sky as the constellation Gemini, ensuring their eternal presence together.
